race.name: Marxa Beret
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through the Pyrenees at 1,800 meters — Spain's improbable Nordic masterpiece in the Catalan heartland of Val d'Aran.

vitals.date: First or second weekend in February
vitals.aid_stations: Stations with Spanish and Aranese mountain hospitality

climate.description: Baqueira Beret sits at 1,500-1,800m in the central Pyrenees, catching Atlantic moisture that delivers heavy snowfall. The Val d'Aran is unique in the Pyrenees — its north-facing Atlantic drainage pattern creates a microclimate more similar to the French side. February temperatures range from -10°C to 2°C.
challenges: High altitude at 1,500-1,800m affects unacclimatized skiers
Pyrenean weather can shift rapidly between sun and storm
Atlantic-influenced snow can be heavier and wetter than Alpine powder
Challenging terrain with meaningful climbing at altitude

course.primary: Loop course through the high Pyrenean terrain around Baqueira Beret, Spain's most prestigious ski resort. The course traverses Alpine meadows and mountain forest at 1,500-1,800m altitude with significant climbing. Classic technique only — the terrain rewards strong diagonal stride on the climbs.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through high Pyrenean terrain
course.grooming: Good grooming at Baqueira Beret's Nordic facilities
features: Spain's biggest and most prestigious XC ski race
Val d'Aran — the only Atlantic-draining valley in the Spanish Pyrenees
Aranese culture — Occitan-speaking valley with unique Pyrenean identity
Baqueira Beret is Spain's premier ski resort, favored by Spanish royalty

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The most unlikely Nordic race on the calendar — a serious XC marathon in the Spanish Pyrenees. The Val d'Aran setting is spectacular, and the Ski Classics connection brings genuine competitive depth. Spain's sole entry in international marathon skiing.

history.summary: Founded in 1981, the Marxa Beret is Spain's premier cross-country ski race, held in the Val d'Aran — a unique Occitan-speaking valley in the central Pyrenees. The race takes place around Baqueira Beret, Spain's most prestigious ski resort and a favorite of the Spanish royal family. Its inclusion in the Ski Classics Challengers series has elevated its international profile.
notable_facts: Spain's biggest and most prestigious cross-country ski race
Val d'Aran is the only valley in Spain that drains toward the Atlantic (via the Garonne)
Aranese, a dialect of Occitan, is co-official with Catalan and Spanish in the valley
Baqueira Beret is favored by the Spanish royal family for winter holidays
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
The Pyrenees at this longitude receive heavy Atlantic snowfall — excellent snow record
